jokes.text: Dennis dramatically announces he's ready for questions about his absence, but no one has been wondering about his personal life
jokes.setup: Dennis builds up this grand moment where he'll answer burning questions about his mysterious absence
jokes.punchline: The gang clearly doesn't care at all and has no questions
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dennis narcissism - he assumes everyone cares about his drama when they're completely indifferent

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Dennis dramatically announces he's ready for questions about his absence, but no one has been wondering about his personal life

jokes.text: The gang's completely disinterested responses: 'I don't care. Do you care?' 'I mean, it doesn't really...'
jokes.setup: Dennis has been building up this dramatic reveal
jokes.punchline: Complete apathy from his supposed friends
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan delivery that deflates Dennis's grandiose moment

dialogue.line: I don't care. Do you care?
I mean, it doesn't really...

jokes.text: 'Sometimes it's best to raise a family from a distance. And that's what I've decided to do, okay? And I'm not the first man in history to ever do it.'
jokes.setup: Dennis trying to justify abandoning his family
jokes.punchline: Framing deadbeat dad behavior as a noble parenting philosophy
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark comedy that perfectly captures Dennis's sociopathic rationalization of terrible behavior

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Sometimes it's best to raise a family from a distance. And that's what I've decided to do, okay? And I'm not the first man in history to ever do it.

jokes.text: 'I mean, you guys wouldn't really understand 'cause you've never owned a vehicle.' 'We own vehicles.' 'Yeah? Name one.' 'Well, we had those matching Mongoose bikes back in the day.'
jokes.setup: Dennis condescendingly says they don't understand car ownership
jokes.punchline: Their 'vehicles' are childhood bicycles
jokes.explanation: Good setup/payoff that highlights the gang's arrested development and lack of adult accomplishments

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: I mean, you guys wouldn't really understand 'cause you've never owned a vehicle.
We own vehicles.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Yeah? Name one.
Well, we had those matching Mongoose bikes back in the day.

jokes.text: Frank gives Dennis $473 - 'That's the Kelley Blue Book value of your car'
jokes.setup: Dennis needs financing for a Range Rover
jokes.punchline: Frank's 'help' is the pathetic trade-in value of Dennis's junker
jokes.explanation: Frank's miserly nature creates a good deflation moment for Dennis's grandiose plans

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: That's the Kelley Blue Book value of your car

jokes.text: You never bought me a Range Rover. All I got were shit cars, which you guys crashed, and you certainly never reimbursed me.
jokes.setup: Frank offering Dennis financing help
jokes.punchline: Dee pointing out the gang's history of destroying her property
jokes.explanation: Good character moment establishing the gang's treatment of Dee

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: You never bought me a Range Rover. All I got were shit cars, which you guys crashed, and you certainly never reimbursed me.

jokes.text: 'Your driver's license is expired.' 'Since when?' '1984.'
jokes.setup: Frank confidently trying to buy a car
jokes.punchline: His license has been expired for over 30 years
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup/payoff with great economy. The specificity of 1984 makes it absurdly funny

dialogue.speaker: Car Salesman
dialogue.line: Your driver's license is expired.

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: Since when?

jokes.text: Dork. / Jump this manhole. / Nice. / Oh, still got the skid.
jokes.setup: Two middle-aged men on bikes
jokes.punchline: They're doing childish bike tricks and calling each other names
jokes.explanation: Good visual comedy of grown men acting like kids

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Dork.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Jump this manhole.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Nice.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Oh, still got the skid.

jokes.text: 'So Shawn Dumont didn't just steal our bikes. He stole our identity.'
jokes.setup: Reflecting on childhood bike theft
jokes.punchline: Dramatically elevating stolen bikes to identity theft
jokes.explanation: Good escalation that sets up the episode's theme while showing Mac's tendency to overdramatize

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: So Shawn Dumont didn't just steal our bikes. He stole our identity.

jokes.text: Kids mock them: 'You guys look like you're 40.' 'Well, we are 40.' 'Gay!' 'Oh, well, I am gay, but he's not my boyfriend, 'cause I could do much better than him.'
jokes.setup: Kids using 'gay' as an insult
jokes.punchline: Mac's matter-of-fact response that yes he's gay but Charlie isn't good enough
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment that subverts expectations while staying true to Mac's personality

dialogue.speaker: Kids
dialogue.line: You guys look like you're 40.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Well, we are 40.

dialogue.speaker: Kids
dialogue.line: Gay!

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Oh, well, I am gay, but he's not my boyfriend, 'cause I could do much better than him.

jokes.text: Oh, well, well, I am gay, but he's not my boyfriend, 'cause I could do much better than him.
jokes.setup: Kid calling them gay
jokes.punchline: Mac confirms he's gay but insults Charlie in the process
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Mac's response is both honest and insulting

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Oh, well, well, I am gay, but he's not my boyfriend, 'cause I could do much better than him.

jokes.text: Dennis hitting on a housekeeper thinking she doesn't speak English, then discovering she does
jokes.setup: Dennis assumes the housekeeper doesn't speak English
jokes.punchline: His awkward realization when she responds perfectly in English
jokes.explanation: Exposes Dennis's casual racism and assumptions in a cringey way

jokes.text: What up, ding-dong?
jokes.setup: Dennis trying to be cool
jokes.punchline: His awkward attempt at casual greeting
jokes.explanation: Mild character moment of Dennis trying too hard

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: What up, ding-dong?

jokes.text: 'I tell you what, I am loving it. And Brenda, you got to upgrade, you stupid bitch. Okay? Don't be a dumb idiot.'
jokes.setup: Dee trying to fit in with wealthy women
jokes.punchline: Her aggressive insults disguised as friendly advice
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dee moment showing her inability to modulate her aggressive nature even when trying to be social

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: I tell you what, I am loving it. And Brenda, you got to upgrade, you stupid bitch. Okay? Don't be a dumb idiot.

jokes.text: Frank's driving advice: 'There's nothing in this book about the Asians. You can't be PC on the road and expect to live.'
jokes.setup: Frank giving driving test advice
jokes.punchline: His casually racist 'wisdom' about Asian drivers
jokes.explanation: Classic Frank casual bigotry presented as practical advice

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: There's nothing in this book about the Asians. You can't be PC on the road and expect to live.

jokes.text: Bruh, that shit was hilarious. You're gonna fail so hard, man.
jokes.setup: Frank's terrible driving advice
jokes.punchline: Teen's accurate assessment that Frank will fail
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective reaction to Frank's incompetence

dialogue.speaker: Teen
dialogue.line: Bruh, that shit was hilarious. You're gonna fail so hard, man.

jokes.text: Am I your Uber? No, I... / Oh, okay. / Yeah, I think I see what's happening here.
jokes.setup: Random person gets in Dennis's car thinking it's Uber
jokes.punchline: Dennis's economic car makes him look like a driver
jokes.explanation: Good visual gag about how the car affects perception

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Am I your Uber? No, I...
Oh, okay.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Yeah, I think I see what's happening here.

jokes.text: That's a strong economy name. You know, it says so much, yet it says so little.
jokes.setup: Dennis learning the stranger's name is John
jokes.punchline: His pretentious analysis of an ordinary name
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment - Dennis's pretentious intellectualizing of the most basic name

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: That's a strong economy name. You know, it says so much, yet it says so little.

jokes.text: That's a strong economy name. You know, it says so much, yet it says so little. ... Yeah, you got a good economy face on you, too.
jokes.setup: Dennis talking to John who mistook him for an Uber driver
jokes.punchline: Dennis analyzing someone's name and face as 'economical'
jokes.explanation: Dennis's weird psychology applied to judging people's 'economy' - very character-specific

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: That's a strong economy name. You know, it says so much, yet it says so little. ... Yeah, you got a good economy face on you, too.

jokes.text: 'Whoa, whoa, whoa, whoa. I got a Chuck Knoblauch!' 'That's, like, pre-yips, man.' 'Pre-yips?'
jokes.setup: Finding old baseball cards
jokes.punchline: The oddly specific reference to Chuck Knoblauch's throwing problems
jokes.explanation: Good sports reference that shows their arrested development and nostalgia

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Whoa, whoa, whoa, whoa. I got a Chuck Knoblauch!

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: That's, like, pre-yips, man.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Pre-yips?

jokes.text: Whoa, whoa, whoa, whoa. I got a Chuck Knoblauch! That's, like, pre-yips, man. / Pre-yips? / That could be worth a fortune!
jokes.setup: Mac and Charlie buying baseball cards
jokes.punchline: Treating a mediocre player's card as valuable based on when it was made
jokes.explanation: Great absurd logic - the idea that timing makes a bad player valuable

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Whoa, whoa, whoa, whoa. I got a Chuck Knoblauch! That's, like, pre-yips, man.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Pre-yips?

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: That could be worth a fortune!

jokes.text: Dennis telling a story to the housekeeper who responds 'It's just not funny.' 'I speak English. It's just not funny.'
jokes.setup: Dennis assuming the housekeeper doesn't understand English
jokes.punchline: Her blunt assessment that his story simply isn't funny
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback with perfect deadpan delivery that completely deflates Dennis's ego

jokes.text: Dee's aggressive rant: 'Oh, Karen, you bitch. You dumb idiot. You stupid savage.'
jokes.setup: Dee being excluded from wealthy women's conversation
jokes.punchline: Her unhinged string of insults
jokes.explanation: Classic Dee meltdown showing her inability to handle social rejection

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: Oh, Karen, you bitch. You dumb idiot. You stupid savage.

jokes.text: Dennis's awkward attempts to hit on Tara, mentioning farts and twins
jokes.setup: Dennis trying to demonstrate his 'mojo'
jokes.punchline: His completely tone-deaf conversation about digestive issues and siblings
jokes.explanation: Great cringe comedy showing Dennis's pickup skills have deteriorated to nothing

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Dennis's awkward attempts to hit on Tara, mentioning farts and twins

jokes.text: You lose your mojo? / I didn't lose my mojo. / Yeah, you got it. / You got the moves? / I got my mojo.
jokes.setup: Teenager questioning Dennis's confidence
jokes.punchline: Dennis desperately defending his 'mojo' to a kid
jokes.explanation: Good cringe of Dennis's insecurity exposed by a teenager

dialogue.speaker: Teen
dialogue.line: You lose your mojo?

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: I didn't lose my mojo.

dialogue.speaker: Teen
dialogue.line: Yeah, you got it.

dialogue.speaker: Teen
dialogue.line: You got the moves?

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: I got my mojo.

jokes.text: Dude, I'll suck you. / You'll suck me? / No, that's... That's not what I meant.
jokes.setup: Dennis saying he doesn't suck
jokes.punchline: Accidental sexual innuendo and immediate backpedal
jokes.explanation: Simple wordplay cringe moment

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Dude, I'll suck you.
You'll suck me?

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: No, that's... That's not what I meant.

jokes.text: What are you, what are you gonna do with that, dude? ... Go hit on the girl, will you? ... I'll show you what sucks, huh?
jokes.setup: Frank filming Dennis and pressuring him to prove his skills
jokes.punchline: Dennis reluctantly agreeing to perform under pressure
jokes.explanation: Setup for Dennis's inevitable humiliation in front of the camera

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: What are you, what are you gonna do with that, dude?

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: Go hit on the girl, will you?

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: I'll show you what sucks, huh?

jokes.text: Oh, no. It makes me... makes me fart. I don't want to, I don't want to fart right now.
jokes.setup: Dennis trying to flirt with girl cooking
jokes.punchline: His extremely unsexy reason for declining food
jokes.explanation: Good cringe flirting - Dennis sabotaging himself with TMI

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Oh, no. It makes me... makes me fart. I don't want to, I don't want to fart right now.

jokes.text: Yeah, she farts a shit ton, man. / Yeah. I-I don't have that. Like, everything makes her... everything makes her fart.
jokes.setup: Dennis trying to connect through twin sister mention
jokes.punchline: He immediately starts talking about his sister's digestive issues
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the awkward flirting into TMI territory

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Yeah, she farts a shit ton, man. / Yeah. I-I don't have that. Like, everything makes her... everything makes her fart.

jokes.text: 'What are you gonna do about it?' leading to Mac and Charlie being unable to respond
jokes.setup: Adult confrontation over the stolen bike
jokes.punchline: The same line that defeated them as children still works 30 years later
jokes.explanation: Excellent payoff to the setup earlier in the episode. The line's continued effectiveness is both funny and tragic

jokes.text: Dee's unhinged declaration: 'I am a golden goddess, you idiot. Savage idiots! And I banged your man.'
jokes.setup: Dee trying to assert dominance over wealthy women
jokes.punchline: Her completely delusional self-image and crude boasting
jokes.explanation: Peak Dee delusion showing her complete misreading of social situations

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: I am a golden goddess, you idiot. Savage idiots! And I banged your man.

jokes.text: The revelation that Aidan is Karen's teenage son, not her boy toy husband
jokes.setup: Dee thinks she's cuckolding Karen with her young husband
jokes.punchline: She actually slept with Karen's underage son
jokes.explanation: Dark twist that completely recontextualizes Dee's 'victory' into something horrifying

dialogue.line: The revelation that Aidan is Karen's teenage son, not her boy toy husband

jokes.text: Oh, my God. You're the creepy old man from Aidan's Instagram stories.
jokes.setup: Karen recognizing Frank
jokes.punchline: Frank's appearance in teenager's social media as 'creepy old man'
jokes.explanation: Good callback that ties together the storylines with consequences

dialogue.speaker: Karen
dialogue.line: Oh, my God. You're the creepy old man from Aidan's Instagram stories.

jokes.text: Jonathan! / What's up? / What is this? What am I seeing?
jokes.setup: Dennis seeing John with his old car
jokes.punchline: Dennis's shock at the reversal of their positions
jokes.explanation: Good reversal showing the arbitrary nature of car-based status

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Jonathan!

dialogue.speaker: John
dialogue.line: What's up?

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: What is this? What am I seeing?

jokes.text: Be gone from me now. / What? / Be gone from me, you soy boy beta cuck.
jokes.setup: Dennis getting his Range Rover back
jokes.punchline: His immediate transformation into an arrogant dismissive person
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Dennis immediately becomes toxic when he gets status back

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Be gone from me now.
What?

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Be gone from me, you soy boy beta cuck.

jokes.text: We did a bad thing, dude. I think I killed a kid. / He did. I saw him. I think the kid's dead.
jokes.setup: Mac and Charlie fleeing from something
jokes.punchline: Casual admission of potentially killing someone
jokes.explanation: Dark humor escalation typical of the show's extreme consequences

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: We did a bad thing, dude. I think I killed a kid.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: He did. I saw him. I think the kid's dead.

jokes.text: Frank admitting he almost got T-boned by an Asian driver and it was 'totally my fault. She did everything by the book. Very surprising.'
jokes.setup: Frank's earlier racist driving advice
jokes.punchline: Reality contradicting his prejudices while he remains oblivious
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback that exposes Frank's racism while he remains completely unaware of the irony

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: I almost got T-boned by an Asian driver and it was totally my fault. She did everything by the book. Very surprising.
